title: An entosis-anchored hybrid prognostic signature identifies immunotherapy-resistant subgroups in head and neck squamous cell carcinoma.
abstract: Entosis is a non-apoptotic form of cell-in-cell death implicated in tumor progression and immune evasion. However, the prognostic and immunological significance of entosis-related genes in head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC) has not been systematically studied. This study aimed to construct and validate an entosis-anchored hybrid prognostic signature for HNSCC and to characterize its association with the tumor immune microenvironment and therapeutic response.
trial_name: Phase II studies of cisplatin and gemcitabine
